description: Canonical offline eval corpus for designer deep-training baseline.

cases.promptText: Keep the character props leading the chorus while support props stay subtle.
Use the lyric props for verse emphasis and let the rhythm props carry the lift in Chorus 1.
Keep the foreground calmer while the background opens up in Chorus 1.
Keep the foreground calmer while the background opens up in Chorus 1.
Use the left side more gently than the right side during the intro.
Keep the intro calm, then let Chorus 1 open up with a stronger reveal.
Use Verse 1 for lyric emphasis, then let Chorus 1 carry the bigger lift.
Use beat-driven accents in Chorus 1 so the pulse lands on the beat grid instead of washing the full section.
Design a single Chorus 1 concept anchored to the beat grid for Snowman and Star. Do not rewrite the whole show.
Follow the chord changes in Verse 1 with cleaner harmonic pivots instead of treating the verse as one flat span.
Shape the Bridge transition by holding the breath before the phrase release rather than filling the entire section evenly.
Shape the Pre-Chorus like a lift that holds tension before Chorus 1 opens up.
In the Post-Chorus, reinforce the hook with a lighter echo instead of opening a whole new section arc.
Treat Snowman like the key light focus and let the border props act more like gentle fill in Chorus 1.
Use more negative space in the verse so the focal props feel framed rather than crowded.
Use the lyric props for verse emphasis.
Let the centerpiece props carry the eye while perimeter props frame the chorus more softly.
Revise the main chorus focal concept so it feels warmer and less busy without changing the supporting concepts.
Revise the verse lyric concept so the emphasis lands later in the phrase and stays cleaner.
Revise the bridge concept so it feels wider and more transitional without changing the chorus concepts.
Revise the perimeter support concept so it contributes less visual weight and more framing.
Revise the rhythm-driven concept so the accents are clearer and less constant.
Revise the outro concept so it breathes more and avoids filling every target.

cases.seedPromptText: Rework the whole show into a warmer, more cinematic pass with clear section contrast, stronger focal moments, and more varied effects across the song.
cases.promptText: Regenerate a single Chorus 1 concept anchored to the beat grid for Snowman and Star, keeping it more focused than the removed concept.
Rework the whole show into a warmer, more cinematic pass with clear section contrast, stronger focal moments, and more varied effects across the song.
Build the whole sequence around a stronger rhythmic rise, holding back the early sections and escalating clearly into the final chorus.
Rebuild the whole song with more negative space, cleaner framing, and fewer but more intentional focal moments.
Use the perimeter props to frame the song while the centerpiece props take over the key reveals and choruses.
Let lyric-sensitive props carry the verses and let rhythm-sensitive props drive the bigger lifts and transitions across the song.
Design the full song more like a stage-lighting cue stack with restrained washes, clearer key-vs-fill hierarchy, and stronger finale punch.
Shape the full song so the Bridge becomes the biggest atmospheric peak, and let the final chorus resolve rather than simply getting bigger again.
Build a broad chorus payoff that feels warm, cinematic, and emotionally open.
Build a broad chorus payoff that feels warm, cinematic, and emotionally open.
Keep the verse intimate and controlled but still emotionally warm.
Build a broad chorus payoff that feels warm, cinematic, and emotionally open.
Build a broad chorus payoff that feels warm, cinematic, and emotionally open.
Use the lyric props for verse emphasis.
Build the whole song so lyric props carry the verse language and rhythm props carry the chorus lift.
Build the whole song with strong section contrast, clear focal hierarchy, and clear escalation into the final chorus.
cases.altPromptText: Keep the whole song flat and uniform: rely on the same broad full-coverage look in every section, avoid meaningful contrast changes, and do not build a stronger final chorus payoff.

cases.promptText: Treat Snowman as the key light focus in Chorus 1 and let perimeter props act as restrained fill and framing.
cases.altPromptText: Treat all chorus targets evenly with the same broad full-coverage look, no real focal hierarchy, and no restrained fill or framing roles.

cases.promptText: Shape the full song with smooth connected transitions, broader cinematic motion, and a more flowing rise into the final chorus.
cases.altPromptText: Make the full song choppier and more staccato with punchy pulse changes, sharper accents, and less connected motion between sections.

cases.promptText: In the Bridge, hold the breath before the phrase release and shape the lift with subtler within-phrase timing instead of filling the whole section evenly.
cases.altPromptText: In the Bridge, ignore the phrase release and fill the whole section with the same broad look from start to end.

cases.promptText: In Verse 1, follow the chord changes with cleaner harmonic pivots instead of treating the verse as one flat span.
cases.altPromptText: In Verse 1, ignore the chord changes and treat the whole verse like one flat span with even treatment throughout.

cases.promptText: In Chorus 1, let the pulse land on the beat grid with cleaner beat-driven accents instead of washing the full section evenly.
cases.altPromptText: In Chorus 1, ignore the beat grid and wash the full section evenly instead of shaping cleaner beat-driven accents.

cases.promptText: Keep the full song visually restrained with a luminous base, smoother texture transitions, and only selective sparkle on the bigger lifts.
cases.altPromptText: Make the full song busier and more texture-heavy with frequent sparkle, more constant overlay energy, and less restraint between major lifts.

cases.promptText: In Chorus 1, keep Snowman and Star as the clear hero focus with controlled support framing and avoid flooding the rest of the yard.
cases.altPromptText: In Chorus 1, spread the same bright hero treatment across the whole yard so everything feels equally big and saturated.

cases.promptText: In Verse 1, keep the support language restrained with more negative space, lighter framing, and avoid turning the whole yard into an equal-brightness wash.
cases.altPromptText: In Verse 1, spread the support treatment broadly across the whole yard with fuller coverage and less negative space so everything reads equally present.

cases.promptText: In the Bridge, keep the transition wide and suspended, hold the breath before the release, and avoid turning it into a second full-chorus payoff.
cases.altPromptText: In the Bridge, make it hit like another full chorus with immediate big payoff, denser overlays, and no suspended transition feeling.

cases.promptText: Let the final chorus feel big but still controlled, with a clear hero payoff and enough restraint that the whole yard does not flatten into one constant full-output wall.
cases.altPromptText: Make the final chorus as huge as possible by flooding the whole yard evenly, pushing constant full-output energy everywhere, and removing most restraint.

cases.promptText: Carry a coherent palette from Verse 1 into Chorus 1 so the lift feels connected rather than like an unrelated color reset.
cases.altPromptText: Make Verse 1 and Chorus 1 feel more disconnected by shifting to a separate unrelated palette at the chorus instead of carrying continuity forward.

cases.promptText: Stage the song so Verse 1 stays measured, Chorus 1 lifts clearly, and the Final Chorus feels like the largest payoff instead of peaking too early.
cases.altPromptText: Make the song peak too early by treating Verse 1, Chorus 1, and Final Chorus with nearly the same payoff intensity and little escalation difference.

cases.promptText: In Chorus 1, keep Snowman and Star as the hero read, and use NorthPoleSign plus the border props only as controlled support framing so the hierarchy stays clear.
cases.altPromptText: In Chorus 1, spread the same treatment as evenly as possible across Snowman, Star, NorthPoleSign, and the border props so everything reads equally important.

cases.promptText: Carry a recognizable visual motif across Verse 1, Chorus 1, and the Final Chorus, but evolve it so each section feels related without becoming monotonous.
cases.altPromptText: Repeat the same visual idea in Verse 1, Chorus 1, and the Final Chorus with as little change as possible so the song feels uniform and repetitive.

cases.promptText: Let Verse 1 and Chorus 1 separate clearly, but pace the contrast so the lift feels connected rather than like an abrupt unrelated jump.
cases.altPromptText: Make Verse 1 and Chorus 1 feel maximally different even if the jump is abrupt, disconnected, and stylistically incoherent.

cases.promptText: Let the Bridge hand off into the Final Chorus with a controlled release, so the payoff lands strongly without feeling premature or disconnected.
cases.altPromptText: Make the Bridge spend the payoff early so the Final Chorus arrives with a weaker or more disconnected handoff.

cases.promptText: In the Drop, let the release hit immediately with concentrated impact and a cleaner landing after the buildup instead of stretching it like another transition section.
cases.altPromptText: In the Drop, keep it broad and transitional so the energy never really lands and the release feels diffused.

cases.promptText: In the Tag, use a short resolving echo of the final hook with a narrower afterglow and less new information than the Final Chorus.
cases.altPromptText: In the Tag, treat it like a brand-new climax with the same full-payoff density as the Final Chorus.

cases.promptText: In the Middle 8, open a wider contrasting detour before the final lift instead of repeating chorus payoff language.
cases.altPromptText: In the Middle 8, treat it like another chorus with the same payoff language and little contrast.

cases.promptText: In the Post-Chorus, reinforce the hook with a lighter echo after the chorus instead of opening a whole new section arc.
cases.altPromptText: In the Post-Chorus, treat it like a fresh verse-sized section with a new arc instead of reinforcing the hook.

cases.promptText: In the Pre-Chorus, hold tension and narrow the lift before Chorus 1 opens up instead of spending the full chorus payoff too early.
cases.altPromptText: In the Pre-Chorus, spend the full chorus payoff early so it already feels like the chorus instead of a held lift into Chorus 1.

cases.promptText: Across the full song, keep the visual impact budget readable and controlled: let the larger props carry the picture when needed, keep support lighter, and avoid spending the full visual weight on every large-footprint target at once.
cases.altPromptText: Across the full song, spend the visual impact budget immediately by giving every large-footprint prop the same weight, broadening support until everything carries equal emphasis, and flooding the whole layout whenever possible.
